evals.prompt: Our checkout has three steps. 40% leave after address, 15% after shipping method, 25% after payment (of those who started). What should we do?
evals.expected_output: Funnel map; branch table with shipping comp for address-heavy, BNPL/trust for payment; resistance classification; 4+ row matrix; mandatory three emails labeled Urgency, Trust rebuild, Ultimatum with A/B subjects.
expectations: Maps or references a three-step checkout model (contact/address, shipping, payment)
Applies branch logic: stronger shipping compensation or clarity emphasis when address-step drop is high; installment/BNPL or payment trust when payment-step drop is notable
Includes a section or equivalent for resistance classification
Includes a Markdown table with at least four rows linking checkout step to resistance, tactic, and email reinforcement
Includes exactly three email blocks labeled with Urgency, Trust rebuild, and Ultimatum (or exact bracket labels [Urgency], [Trust rebuild], [Ultimatum])
Each of the three emails includes two subject line variants and a body skeleton with CTA and compliance-friendly language

evals.prompt: People abandon checkout a lot. That's it.
evals.expected_output: Pushy: still outputs three-part Urgency/Trust/Ultimatum emails; full framework for all three steps with instrumentation if no data.
expectations: Still provides the three-part email sequence with Urgency, Trust rebuild, and Ultimatum themes
Does not refuse email scripts solely because the user did not ask for them
Covers all three checkout steps with default branch hypotheses when step data is absent

evals.expected_output: Brief WISMO style; no full checkout recovery funnel or three-email mandate.
expectations: Does not output the full mandatory three-theme abandoned-checkout email sequence as the primary deliverable
Does not force step-based shipping or BNPL compensation strategy for a tracking-only request
